workflow round 1: 9-variant exploration of non-cheat structural levers from score-12 base; UB-form proof that target's $a3 allocation IS C-reachable via a forbidden pattern
sessions.note: Baseline confirmed: score=12, build_insns=50, target_insns=51. Explored 9 distinct legitimate levers ŌĆö all measured negative. Notable new evidence: the C86C-sibling UB-form pattern (`var_a1 = arg1` ONLY in the negative-arg0 else branch, leaving var_a1 read uninitialized on positive arg0 path) DOES emit target's `move a3, a0` X-preserve allocation in the assembly ŌĆö proving the $a3 allocation IS structurally reachable. However, that form scores 18 (worse than 12 on masked Levenshtein, even with build_insns matching target's 51) AND is a literal-rename-style coercion per [[param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip]] ŌĆö forbidden as cheat-by-spelling, same category as the previously-rejected raw_arg0 chain. So even though the $a3 register match exists in the explored search space, every path to it requires a UB or coercion pattern excluded by the completion standard.

Per [[no-new-park-categories]] ┬¦ 'Auto-search tools ŌĆö output is PROPOSALS, not winners' all correctly rejected. Corroborates round-1 UB-form proof: search space contains masked-Levenshtein-closer shapes, but every closer shape needs UB OR semantic change OR coercion-only synthetic local. Engine bug noted: `engine.inlineasm.write_stripped` strips `#define PAD_NOPS_*` macros in display.c, leaving raw `PAD_NOPS_1;` references that maspsx can't parse; worked around with `--keep-cheat-asm` flag (func has no cheat-asm pins so score unchanged).

(T2) precompute `var_v0 = var_v0_2;` identity BEFORE dispatch, then mask in each branch ŌĆö score 22 build_insns 52 (regression, sibling of round-2's score-95 synthetic-local family already in rejected_forms). sessions.lever: round 5 NO_PROGRESS: bgtz vs bgez X-sign-check opcode swap; semantically equivalent (arg0==0 falls through else either way) but unrejected to date
sessions.note: Single-variant probe from candidate score-12 base. Verified candidate floor 12 reproducible at HEAD 19c0c8c3 (build_insns=50, target 51, 21 rules dropped, 452 cheat-asm-stripped) ŌĆö the round-4 regression (HEAD body scoring 20 via synthetic new_var/new_var2 named-intermediates) is unchanged. Variant T1-round5: `if (arg0 > 0)` instead of `if (arg0 >= 0)` for the X-sign check. Semantically equivalent ŌĆö when arg0 == 0, candidate's `arg0 >= 0` takes the true branch with X-clamp (`D-1 < 0` false, `var_v0_2 = arg0 = 0`); my variant `arg0 > 0` takes the else branch with `var_v0_2 = 0`. Identical observable result. Different RTL opcode emission: `bgtz` vs `bgez`. Measured: score 13 (regression +1 vs candidate's 12). The bgtz form lengthens the X-clamp's RTL chain by one branch-test variant the masked-Levenshtein metric counts as an additional diff. Source reverted. Adds one more datapoint to the now-31-distinct-variant rejection corpus for this function (round-1: 9 + round-2 saved permuter outputs: 11 + round-4: 3 + this: 1 = 24 manual + permuter-class derivatives; plus the ~60k+ permuter iters across rounds 1-2). PARK_CANDIDATE recommendation per next_hypotheses[0].

sessions.lever: round 7 NO_PROGRESS: un-decremented limit-save (lim_x=D_8009BE78 named local) + var_v0_2=arg0 default-init with bgez-first compare
sessions.note: Candidate baseline 12 reconfirmed at HEAD 8b45967d (build_insns 50 vs target 51, 21 rules dropped, 452 cheat-asm stripped). Two new manual variants tested, both NEGATIVE: (T1-round7) save un-decremented limits in named locals ŌĆö `s16 lim_x = D_8009BE78; s16 lim_y = D_8009BE7A;` ŌĆö and reference `(lim_x - 1) < arg0` / `var_v0_2 = lim_x - 1` etc. Hypothesis: mirror target's `addu $a2, $v0, $zero` raw-limit save (target preserves the un-decremented D_8009BE78 in $a2 to support the `addiu $v0, $a2, -1` delay-slot fill). Score 12 (NO CHANGE) ŌĆö GCC CSEs lim_x back into the same RTL pseudo as the direct `D_8009BE78` read; the named-local intermediate is allocation-neutral. Distinct from rejected_forms[6] which saved the DECREMENTED limit (`s16 xlim = D_8009BE78 - 1`, score 20). (T2-round7) initialize var_v0_2 = arg0 default at function entry as a structural mirror of target's first-instruction `addu $a3, $a0, $zero` (target preserves raw arg0 into $a3, then $a3 becomes the holder for the clamped var_v0_2 via subsequent overwrite). Structure: `var_v0_2 = arg0; if (arg0 >= 0) { if ((D_8009BE78-1) < arg0) var_v0_2 = D_8009BE78-1; /* else: var_v0_2 stays as arg0 */ } else { var_v0_2 = 0; }` ŌĆö distinct from rejected_forms[2] which used bltz-first compare sense at score 20; mine is bgez-first matching the empirically-confirmed local-optimum branch opcode from round 5. Score 16 (regression +4 vs candidate). The default-init pattern interacts with the bgez-first nested structure such that GCC's RTL collapses the merge differently than candidate's explicit else-branch; the explicit `var_v0_2 = arg0` else-branch in the candidate's form IS load-bearing for floor 12 (cannot be replaced by a default-init relying on no-op-when-in-range). Source reverted; oracle confirmed green (build SHA1 == 62efab4f). HEAD drift NOTE: meta.json head_floor was 16 when written; current HEAD scores 20 (synthetic new_var/new_var2 named-intermediates per round-4 finding); updated to 20. Rejection corpus now 30 distinct variants across rounds 1, 4, 5, 6, 7 plus ~60k+ permuter iters rounds 1-2. No new rejected_forms appended (T1 was no-op-equivalent; T2 same category as rejected_forms[2] with branch-sense flipped). PARK_CANDIDATE escalation from rounds 5/6 stands; the lim_x/default-init levers were the documented round-5 `next_hypotheses[0]` 'unrejected lever' candidates outside the SOTN-form question (which round 6 closed). Per [[no-new-park-categories]] user policy, function remains INCOMPLETE; per user round-7 directive PARK_CANDIDATE is not terminal ŌĆö escalation note unchanged: (a) PERM_GENERAL DIRECTED permuter ~30k iters; (b) BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG/PRIODBG instrumented cc1 dump on score-12 form to identify pseudo priorities of arg0/var_v0_2/var_a1; both require multi-session infrastructure not feasible inside one worker run. sessions.lever: round 9 NO_PROGRESS: all 5 v2 novel levers from tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C7A0_v2.md (RTL/sibling cross-reference) tested at candidate baseline ŌĆö Lever 2 (var_v1 precompute in else only), Lever 1 (mode_m1 hoist), Lever 5 (arg1_neg sign hoist), Lever 4 (limits pointer share), Lever 3 (Lever 1+2+drop r_e3 combined). sessions.note: Candidate baseline 12 reconfirmed at HEAD 73f4ce29 (build_insns 50 vs target 51, 21 rules dropped, 452 cheat-asm stripped). Five distinct fresh variants tested, ALL measured NEGATIVE: (T1-round9, Lever 2 block-local precompute) move `var_v1 = var_a1 & 0xFFF` UNCONDITIONAL precompute INTO the else (wide-mode) arm only ŌĆö score 18 (regression +6); build_insns now 51 matching target, but the shrunken var_v1 livelen widens the masked-Lev cascade rather than narrowing it. The hypothesis that var_v1's two-set livelen was biasing a callee-save was disproven ŌĆö the single-set form in just the wide arm produces a worse masked diff. (T2-round9, Lever 1 mode hoist) declare `s32 mode_m1 = D_8009BE74 - 1;` at entry, use `mode_m1` in dispatch ŌĆö score 23 (regression +11); build_insns 48 (3 short). The early mode_m1 load adds a pseudo that CSEs back to a single D_8009BE74 reference but its livelen extension over the X/Y clamps shifts allocation in the wrong direction. (T3-round9, Lever 5 arg1_neg precompute) `s32 arg1_neg = arg1 < 0;` precomputed sign for Y-clamp predicate via `!arg1_neg` ŌĆö score 28 (severe regression +16); the comparison-result pseudo competes for $a2 but pushes the cascade further in the wrong direction. The Y sign-extend pseudo target.s emits at idx 14-15 is not synthesizable via a comparison hoist. (T4-round9, Lever 4 pointer share) `s16 *limits = &D_8009BE78;` then `limits[0]-1`, `limits[1]-1` ŌĆö score 18 (regression +6); the limits pointer materialization adds insns without reducing the cascade. CSE collapses both LO16 references back to the same `lui+addiu` either way per round-7 T1 evidence. (T5-round9, Lever 3 combined) Lever 1 + Lever 2 + drop r_e3 named local (`return var_v1 | var_v0 | 0xE3000000;`) ŌĆö score 29 (severe regression +17); regressions compound. The combination thesis (fewer pseudos = different allocno priority sort) was disproven ŌĆö the individually-negative levers add their regressions rather than cancelling. sessions.lever: round 11 NO_PROGRESS: instrumented-cc1 ALLOCDBG/SCHEDDBG/PRIODBG diagnostic on the score-12 candidate body ŌĆö quantified pseudo 72 (X-preserve var_v0_2 backup) as the lowest-priority allocno (pri=1818, ord=10/10) explaining the structural $a2 lock. Five candidate Lever A/B/C variants self-vetted against cheat-reviewer 6-test checklist ŌĆö all REJECTED on inspection (already-tested OR semantic-change OR FORBIDDEN per chain-extender marker).
sessions.note: Ran `tmp/gccdbg/cc1` (SHA1 79fdb5a4569db10e8815cdc05ddad7f8aca4fd66, May 30 build with all three hooks live; canonical `tools/gcc-2.7.2/build/cc1` SHA1 045c9543d39ab8109583b92137c7adde084f7a25 untouched) on `permuter/c7a0_wf341/base.c` (== candidate.c body). `BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG=1 BB2_SCHED_DEBUG=1 BB2_PRIO_DEBUG=1` produced 11 ALLOCDBG entries (the full allocno-sort table) + 13 SCHEDDBG blocks + ~580 PRIODBG records. Dumps preserved at tmp/gccdbg_func_8007C7A0/dumps.log + cc1_raw.s + build.s + diagnosis.md (tmp/ gitignored; diagnosis surfaces the findings into this session entry). KEY FINDINGS: (1) Pseudo 72 maps to the X-preserve `move $6,$4` at cc1_raw.s line 23 ŌĆö the var_v0_2 backup for the else-branch of the X-clamp. Allocator places it in $a2 (hardreg 6). Target wants this in $a3 (hardreg 7, target.s line 84 `addu $a3, $a0, $zero`). (2) Pseudo 72 has pri=1818, the LOWEST in the allocno-sort table (ord=10/10) ŌĆö nrefs=2, livelen=11, floor_log2(2)*2/11*10000 = 1818. By the time it's allocated, ord=0..9 have taken: $v1=pseudo 79 (pri 10769 Y-mask chain), $a0=pseudo 76 (SE-arg0 pri 3703), $a1=pseudo 77 (var_a1 pri 3529), $a2=pseudo 91 (SE-arg1 pri 4285 at livelen 7, non-overlapping with pseudo 72's livelen 11 so they share $a2). First non-conflicting reg is $a2 ŌåÆ pseudo 72 lands there; $a3 is never claimed because allocation walks ascending and stops at the first free slot. (3) Mine vs target structural difference: mine has X-limit-save in $v1 (pseudo 82 at line 35), target has X-limit-save in $a2 (target.s line 92); mine has X-preserve in $a2 (pseudo 72), target has X-preserve in $a3. Target's allocator picked a DIFFERENT $v1-occupant making $v1 unavailable for X-limit-save, which pushed X-limit-save up to $a2, which pushed X-preserve to $a3. (4) To replicate this in cc1's allocator without forbidden cheats requires increasing some OTHER pseudo's priority enough to occupy $a2 across pseudo 72's livelen ŌĆö which is exactly the chain-extender mechanism FORBIDDEN per [[register-alloc-pure-c]] ┬¦6 (2026-06-02 marker, same family as Lever D dead-stores). FIVE candidate Lever A/B/C variants self-vetted against cheat-reviewer 6-test checklist BEFORE coding (per campaign brief ŌĆö `chain-extender forms inspired by the ALLOCDBG output are FORBIDDEN`): (A.1) block-local var_a1 wide-arm-only precompute = SKIP (already v2 Lever 2, rejected_forms[25] score 18); (A.2) block-local var_v0_2 inside wide-arm = FAIL test #1 semantic (X-clamp result is used in BOTH dispatch arms, narrowing to one arm requires duplicating into other = structural cheat); (B.1) narrow var_v0_2/var_a1 to u8 = FAIL test #1 semantic (D_8009BE78/D_8009BE7A halfwords exceed 255, would truncate); (B.2) widen var_v0/var_v1 from s32 to u32 = SKIP (permuter rounds 1-2 ~60k iters exhausted type-mutation space); (C) loop-local precompute = N/A (no loop); (D) goto-form X-clamp = SKIP (already rejected_forms[7] round 1 score 16). NO src edits applied this round; oracle remains green (no src/ touched, no rebuild needed). Per campaign brief: 'the commit is wip: with the diagnosis content + sessions[] entry documenting what the dump revealed + which Lever A/B/C candidates were tested and how they measured. That IS the deliverable.' Rejection corpus unchanged at 33 distinct variants; no new rejected_forms added (the 5 self-vetted candidates above are either duplicates of existing rejected_forms or fail without measurement and don't merit new entries). PARK_CANDIDATE escalation from rounds 5/6/7/9 stands with one MORE datapoint: the instrumented-cc1 dump CORROBORATES the rounds-1-9 empirical evidence at the mechanism level. The remaining genuinely-untried avenues are off-band infrastructure (project-wide rodata reorder per [[no-new-park-categories]] note OR canonical-asm authorization OR accept persistent INCOMPLETE) requiring user policy judgment, not more pure-C lever testing.

sessions.lever: round 12 NO_PROGRESS: 3 novel structural variants from candidate baseline (V1 single-expression return / V2 unsigned X-limit compare / V3 declaration-order reversal) + 12-min/~21,621-iter random permuter from candidate base. All 3 manual variants regressed or held; all 10 sub-baseline permuter candidates self-vetted as forbidden cheat families.
sessions.note: Candidate baseline 12 reconfirmed at HEAD ad09b020 (build_insns 50 vs target 51, 21 rules dropped, 452 cheat-asm stripped). Three novel manual variants tested (NOT in rejected_forms[] corpus through round 11): (V1) single-expression return ŌĆö drop var_v0/var_v1 locals, compute packed return value inline as `((var_a1 & MASK) << SHIFT) | (var_v0_2 & MASK) | 0xE3000000` in each dispatch arm; eliminates the r_e3 block scope AND the precompute. Score 22 (regression +10) ŌĆö collapsing the named intermediates to inline expressions hoists the OR-chain pseudo's livelen across the dispatch, biasing allocation in the wrong direction. (V2) unsigned X-limit compare ŌĆö flip `(D_8009BE78 - 1) < arg0` to `(u32)arg0 > (u32)(D_8009BE78 - 1)` inside the `arg0 >= 0` guard (semantically equivalent since arg0 non-negative); hypothesis: sltu vs slt opcode and operand-order shift per compare-operand-order-register rule may shift RTL emission. Score 13 (regression +1) ŌĆö operand order flip with constant RHS produces a different sltiu lowering path; the constant-RHS case the compare-operand-order rule explicitly excludes is reaffirmed. (V3) declaration order reversal ŌĆö declare locals in reverse order (var_v1, var_v0, var_a1, var_v0_2) vs candidate (var_v0_2, var_a1, var_v0, var_v1); hypothesis: pseudo numbering MIGHT track decl order for autos. Score 12 (no change) ŌĆö confirms per [[register-alloc-pure-c]] cpu_side_move_dir_4 session-4 finding that pseudo numbers for autos don't track decl order; allocation-neutral. Permuter run from candidate base: ~21,621 iters in 12 min wallclock (720s timeout, -j 4, random-mode), saved 10 sub-baseline candidates at permuter-weighted scores 75/90/95/120/150/175/175/180/185/185 (185=baseline). All self-vetted as forbidden per cheat-reviewer 6-test checklist BEFORE proposing: (75) shared-zero routing `var_v1 = 0; var_v0_2 = var_v1;` in neg-arg0 else + UB read `var_a1 = var_v1;` in neg-Y else (var_v1 uninitialized on positive-arg0 path) ŌĆö TWO cheat families compound (rejected_forms[12-14] synthetic-local routing + rejected_forms[16] UB family). (90) `var_a1 = 0;` in neg-arg0 else branch ŌĆö SEMANTIC CHANGE (zeros Y on arg0<0 path), exactly rejected_forms[11]. (120) places `var_a1 = arg1;` ONLY in neg-arg0 else branch, leaving var_a1 read uninitialized on pos-arg0 path ŌĆö exactly rejected_forms[1] (the C86C-sibling UB-form pattern, forbidden per [[param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip]] + UB). (95/150/175/180) all map to round-2 rejected_forms[12-19] permuter-output families (synthetic-local routing, semantic change, UB, named-intermediate-for-boolean) ŌĆö same shapes the round-2 ~8254-iter permuter found. The fresh ~21k-iter random-mode permuter run produced ZERO legitimate sub-baseline candidates. Corroborates rounds-1-11 finding at THIRD independent measurement: the search space DOES contain masked-Levenshtein-closer shapes but every shape requires UB OR semantic change OR coercion-only synthetic local OR named-intermediate-for-boolean. The round-11 ALLOCDBG-quantified structural ceiling stands: pseudo 72 (X-preserve var_v0_2 backup) has pri=1818 (LOWEST in 11-pseudo allocno-sort table); to flip to $a3 requires some other pseudo to own $a2 across pseudo 72's livelen with pri > 1818 ŌĆö exactly the chain-extender mechanism FORBIDDEN per [[register-alloc-pure-c]] ┬¦6 (2026-06-02 marker). Source reverted post-experiments; oracle confirmed green (build SHA1 == 62efab4f73f992798c43e8c730aa43baa10bb4fa). Three new rejected_forms entries appended (V1/V2/V3). Total rejection corpus now 36 distinct manual variants across rounds 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 12 plus ~80k+ cumulative permuter iters (rounds 1-2 ~60k + round 12 ~21k). PARK_CANDIDATE escalation from rounds 5/6/7/9/11 reinforced with: (a) THREE truly novel manual variants empirically rejected this round, (b) a third independent random-mode permuter run produced zero legitimate sub-baseline candidates. The genuinely-untried-and-not-forbidden avenues remain off-band infrastructure (project-wide rodata reorder with structural evidence per [[no-new-park-categories]] checklist OR accept persistent INCOMPLETE with documented evidence ledger). Concrete next action documented in next_hypotheses[0]: evidence-based source-file TU re-attribution analysis of D_8009BE74/D_8009BE78/D_8009BE7A ŌĆö examine whether display.c's current splat grouping reflects the original source's TU boundary OR if these screen-bound globals belong with a different .c file whose link-order would shift the pseudo-allocation landscape. Sibling func_8007C86C carries identical pattern with same NO_PROGRESS outcome expected for these 3 V1/V2/V3 variants.

sessions.lever: round 6 NO_PROGRESS: SOTN-form param-reassignment lever (per fresh 2026-06-04 SOTN research in tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C7A0.md) ŌĆö reassign arg0/arg1 directly instead of via var_v0_2/var_a1 intermediates
sessions.note: Fresh SOTN research today (tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C7A0.md) identified that SOTN's matched twins (get_cs/get_ce/get_ofs in libgpu/sys.c) converged on a code-shape preference: reassign the parameter slots directly (`x = CLAMP(x, ...)`) WITHOUT introducing var_v0_2/var_a1 intermediates. Hypothesis: BB2's intermediates create the simultaneous-liveness window that drives cc1's $a2 selection for X-preserve; reassigning the param directly keeps X in its arg slot, allowing the $a3 allocation. Three variants tested at HEAD e6a049b4, candidate baseline 12 confirmed: (T1-round6, full SOTN form) bltz-first if/else-if/else clamps reassigning BOTH arg0 and arg1 directly ŌĆö score 28 (severe regression, +16 vs candidate). The bltz-first compare-sense flip alone regresses (already documented as rejected_forms[19] round 4 T1 at score 16); combining it with arg0 reassignment compounds. (T2-round6, SOTN-blend) keep candidate's bgez-first nested-if shape BUT reassign arg0 (replacing var_v0_2) AND arg1 (replacing var_a1) ŌĆö score 16 (regression +4). The arg0 reassignment specifically widens the masked-Lev cascade ŌĆö direct param-reassignment doesn't preserve the local-variable RTL pseudo numbering the candidate's var_v0_2 form relies on. (T3-round6, half-SOTN) keep var_v0_2 for arg0, reassign arg1 directly (drop var_a1) ŌĆö score 12 (no change). Dropping the var_a1 intermediate alone is allocation-neutral; var_v0_2's separation from arg0's pseudo IS load-bearing for the floor 12. SOTN research disproven for BB2 ŌĆö the SOTN-converged form does NOT transfer; BB2's score-12 floor REQUIRES the var_v0_2 intermediate that SOTN's matchers explicitly avoided. Three new datapoints (rejected_forms[22-24] added). Total rejection corpus now 28 distinct variants across rounds 1, 4, 5, 6 plus ~60k+ permuter iters rounds 1-2. PARK_CANDIDATE stands ŌĆö the SOTN evidence resolves the unrejected-lever question from round-5 next_hypotheses[0] (the SOTN form is now empirically rejected too).

reviewer.evidence: Not yet invoked on the score-12 form. Manual self-audit by session D's worker against the 6-test checklist: PASS ŌĆö all four lever stages have semantic purpose (var_a1 preload is a real local that holds the Y argument; r_e3 is the GPU command word naming the constant; var_v1 precompute is the SOTN-allowed duplicate-read pattern resolved 2026-06-02; the overwrite in the narrow-mode branch is real semantic logic, not a coercion). Cheat-reviewer should still be invoked for an independent verdict before any Match: commit.

remaining_gap.summary: $a2-vs-$a3 X-preserve register tiebreaker cascade. The missing park insn at .L8007C7E0 plus the X-preserve register choice ($a2 mine vs $a3 target) cascade through ~10 register-rotation diffs. cc1's default ascending allocation order picks $a2 for the X preserve when both $a2 and $a3 are free; target picks $a3. Every legitimate (non-cheat) C lever tested leaves the tiebreaker unchanged.

rejected_forms.form: s16 raw_arg0 = arg0; ... raw_arg0 = var_v0_2; ... use raw_arg0 in mask expressions
rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN per .claude/rules/param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip.md + .claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md. raw_arg0 is a literal rename of arg0 (identical type, identical lvalue, zero semantic information added). The mid-function reassignment raw_arg0 = var_v0_2 is a literal-rename-followed-by-reuse chain solely to coerce GCC's allocator into picking $a3. The justification references GCC internals (allocno priority biasing $a3 vs $a2 tiebreaker) ŌĆö the canonical cheat-by-spelling signal. Cheat-reviewer would FAIL.

rejected_forms.form: s16 var_v0_2; s16 var_a1; ... if (arg0 >= 0) { ... } else { var_v0_2 = 0; var_a1 = arg1; } if (var_a1 >= 0) ... [C86C/HEAD-style UB: var_a1 read uninitialized on positive-arg0 path]
rejected_forms.reason: Score WORSE than candidate's 12 despite build_insns=51 (matches target). DOES produce target's `move a3, a0` X-preserve allocation (proves $a3 is C-reachable) but the masked-diff cascade is larger. Additionally FORBIDDEN per [[param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip]]: var_a1 = arg1 placed ONLY in the negative-arg0 else branch is a coercion to delay arg1's pseudo creation specifically to bend cc1's allocation tiebreaker (same intent as the rejected raw_arg0 chain). Reading var_a1 uninitialized on the positive path is UB the original C author would not have written; it's an m2c artifact pattern. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

The bgtz form lengthens the X-clamp's RTL chain by introducing a strictly-positive comparison whose masked-Levenshtein cost exceeds the bgez form. Confirms bgez (arg0 >= 0) is the local optimum branch opcode for the X-sign check. (Workflow round 5, 2026-06-04, git_head 19c0c8c3.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 6 T1 (SOTN-form full reassign): bltz-first if/else-if/else clamps reassigning BOTH `arg0` and `arg1` directly ŌĆö `if (arg0 < 0) arg0 = 0; else if ((D_8009BE78-1) < arg0) arg0 = D_8009BE78-1;` same for arg1; drop ALL var_v0_2/var_a1 intermediates; mask + dispatch reads `arg0`/`arg1` directly.
rejected_forms.reason: Severe regression (+16 vs candidate's 12). The SOTN-converged form (per tmp/sotn_research_func_8007C7A0.md) does NOT transfer to BB2. Two compounding factors: (a) bltz-first compare-sense flip alone is rejected_forms[19] at score 16 (round 4 T1); (b) direct arg0 reassignment widens the masked-Lev cascade further by collapsing the local-pseudo separation cc1's allocator uses for X-preserve scheduling. (Workflow round 6, 2026-06-04, git_head e6a049b4.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 6 T2 (SOTN-blend): keep candidate's bgez-first nested-if shape but reassign `arg0` (replacing var_v0_2) AND `arg1` (replacing var_a1) ŌĆö preserve candidate's outer if/else structure, just drop both intermediate s16 locals.
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+4 vs candidate's 12). The arg0 ŌåÆ param-reassignment specifically loses the var_v0_2 intermediate's pseudo-number separation that the candidate's score-12 form relies on. Dropping arg1's intermediate alone is harmless (see T3), but dropping var_v0_2 costs 4 masked-Lev diffs. Disproves the SOTN-shape hypothesis: BB2's score-12 floor REQUIRES the var_v0_2 intermediate that SOTN's matchers (get_cs/get_ce/get_ofs) explicitly avoided. (Workflow round 6, 2026-06-04, git_head e6a049b4.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 6 T3 (half-SOTN): keep var_v0_2 for arg0 X-clamp, reassign arg1 directly (drop var_a1 intermediate) ŌĆö `if (arg1 >= 0) { if ((D_8009BE7A-1) < arg1) arg1 = D_8009BE7A-1; } else arg1 = 0;`
rejected_forms.reason: SAME as candidate (no progress). The var_a1 intermediate vs direct arg1 reassignment is allocation-neutral at this floor (GCC's RTL treats the post-clamp arg1 pseudo equivalently to a fresh var_a1 pseudo once both are computed before dispatch). Confirms var_a1 is not load-bearing for floor 12; var_v0_2 IS load-bearing (see T2). Useful for narrowing the structural ceiling explanation but no progress. (Workflow round 6, 2026-06-04, git_head e6a049b4.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 9 T1 (v2 Lever 2 block-local precompute): move `var_v1 = var_a1 & 0xFFF;` UNCONDITIONAL precompute from before dispatch INTO the else (wide-mode) arm only ŌĆö single-set pseudo confined to wide arm; narrow arm unchanged.
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+6 vs candidate). The hypothesis was that var_v1's two-set livelen biased a callee-save slot away from var_v0_2's X-preserve; the single-set form in just the wide arm should free that slot. Empirically wrong: the shortened var_v1 livelen widens the masked-Lev cascade rather than narrowing it. Even though build_insns now equals target's 51 (vs candidate's 50, 1 short), the masked-diff distance is 6 worse ŌĆö the precompute hoisted ABOVE dispatch was load-bearing for the score-12 floor's specific X-preserve assignment pattern. Disproves the SOTN-duplicate-read-can-be-narrower hypothesis from v2 research. (Workflow round 9, 2026-06-05, git_head 73f4ce29.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 9 T2 (v2 Lever 1 mode hoist): declare `s32 mode_m1 = D_8009BE74 - 1;` at function entry, use `mode_m1` in dispatch `if ((u32)mode_m1 >= 2U)` instead of inline `D_8009BE74 - 1`.
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+11 vs candidate). The mode_m1 pseudo's livelen extension over the X/Y clamp blocks was hypothesized to compete with var_v0_2 for $a2, freeing $a3 for X-preserve. Empirically wrong: GCC CSEs mode_m1 back to the inline form (per round-7 T1 limit save pattern's same CSE behavior) AND the byte-global early read shifts allocation in the wrong direction (build_insns drops to 48, 3 short ŌĆö the early load eliminates 3 of the natural-form insns through different scheduling). The pseudo-priority rebalance the v2 mechanism predicted doesn't materialize. (Workflow round 9, 2026-06-05, git_head 73f4ce29.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 9 T3 (v2 Lever 5 arg1_neg sign hoist): `s32 arg1_neg = arg1 < 0;` precomputed at entry, then Y-clamp predicate uses `if (!arg1_neg)` instead of `if (var_a1 >= 0)`.
rejected_forms.reason: Severe regression (+16 vs candidate). The comparison-result pseudo `arg1_neg` was hypothesized to compete for $a2 with the long live range spanning both clamps, leaving $a3 for X-preserve. Empirically wrong: the sign-extend pseudo target.s emits at idx 14-15 is NOT synthesizable via a comparison-result hoist ŌĆö the comparison RTL is materially different from the sign-extend insn pair target emits, and target's pseudo dies at the bltz check while my hoisted `arg1_neg` survives across the X-clamp. Confirms the v2 mechanism's RTL-priority-shift hypothesis was overly optimistic about which RTL ops are equivalent. (Workflow round 9, 2026-06-05, git_head 73f4ce29.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 9 T4 (v2 Lever 4 pointer share): `s16 *limits = &D_8009BE78;` at entry, then `limits[0] - 1` (X-limit) and `limits[1] - 1` (Y-limit) instead of direct globals.
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+6 vs candidate). The limits-pointer share was hypothesized to introduce a NEW pseudo holding the base address that target's idx 7 (`addu $a2, $v0, $zero` = un-decremented limit save) implicitly carries, with a different RTL shape than round-7 T1's named-local CSE collapse. Empirically: CSE collapses both `limits[0]` and `limits[1]` LO16 references back to the same shape as direct global access (both `lui+addiu(0)` / `lui+addiu(2)` materializations), but the introduced pointer pseudo's livelen costs the masked-Lev cascade. Same outcome as round-7 T1's lim_x save ŌĆö the pointer-arithmetic vs scalar-arithmetic distinction the v2 lever predicted is invisible to cc1's RTL after combine. (Workflow round 9, 2026-06-05, git_head 73f4ce29.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 9 T5 (v2 Lever 3 combined): apply Lever 1 (mode_m1 hoist) + Lever 2 (var_v1 precompute in else only) + drop r_e3 named local (`return var_v1 | var_v0 | 0xE3000000;`) simultaneously.
rejected_forms.reason: Severe regression (+17 vs candidate). The combination hypothesis (fewer pseudos at the global allocator ŌåÆ different priority sort ŌåÆ different hard-reg dispositions ŌåÆ X-preserve tips into $a3) was disproven empirically: the individually-negative levers (T2 +11, T1 +6) compound their regressions rather than cancelling. Per session-5/8 ALLOCDBG findings cited in the v2 research, fewer pseudos CAN flip the sort, but only if the removed pseudos were the ones biasing AGAINST target's choice ŌĆö in this case they were biasing TOWARD target's choice (the var_v1 precompute and the mode pseudo's CSE-anchored form were both contributing to the score-12 floor). Closing the v2 research's combined-lever hypothesis. (Workflow round 9, 2026-06-05, git_head 73f4ce29.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 12 V1: single-expression return ŌĆö drop var_v0/var_v1 named locals entirely, compute packed return value inline as `((var_a1 & MASK) << SHIFT) | (var_v0_2 & MASK) | 0xE3000000` in each dispatch arm; also drops the r_e3 block scope AND the SOTN-duplicate-read precompute.
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+10 vs candidate). Eliminating ALL named intermediates in the return collapses the OR-chain into a single multi-pseudo expression whose unified livelen spans both dispatch arms; this biases the allocator differently than the candidate's staged var_v1/var_v0/r_e3 form. The var_v1 precompute that round-9 T1 proved load-bearing for floor 12 is now also missing. (Workflow round 12, 2026-06-05, git_head ad09b020.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 12 V2: unsigned X-limit compare with operand-order flip ŌĆö `(u32)arg0 > (u32)(D_8009BE78 - 1)` instead of `(D_8009BE78 - 1) < arg0` inside the `arg0 >= 0` guard. Semantically equivalent (arg0 non-negative inside guard, both operands non-negative, sltu == slt).
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+1 vs candidate). The [[compare-operand-order-register]] rule explicitly excludes the constant-RHS case (`comparison is against 0` or a constant), and this case confirms: with a constant-RHS limit-1 expression, flipping operand order produces a different sltiu lowering path that doesn't yield the allocator-shift the rule's local-vs-global comparison case produces. (Workflow round 12, 2026-06-05, git_head ad09b020.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 12 V3: declaration-order reversal ŌĆö declare locals as (var_v1, var_v0, var_a1, var_v0_2) instead of candidate's (var_v0_2, var_a1, var_v0, var_v1).
rejected_forms.reason: SAME as candidate (no progress). Per [[register-alloc-pure-c]] cpu_side_move_dir_4 session-4 finding, pseudo numbers for autos do NOT track C-declaration order ŌĆö they follow first-use order in RTL emission. Allocation-neutral. Closes the decl-order hypothesis. (Workflow round 12, 2026-06-05, git_head ad09b020.)

sibling_impact: func_8007C86C has IDENTICAL 21-rule pattern (constant differs: 0xE4 vs 0xE3). The same SOTN duplicate-read lever applies and reaches the same floor 12. See memory/wip/func_8007C86C/.
